raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A general reaction to the passage would centrally involve judging whether the protocol and contribution are framed clearly at the conceptual level.","R1.2":"The delegation invites assessment of whether the methodological description and interpretive conclusion follow coherently from the stated setup.","R1.3":"Because the excerpt reports accuracy ranges and a correlation-based implication, judging whether the claims are aligned with the reported evidence is central.","R1.5":"A general opinion on a draft section includes whether the ordering of protocol, dataset construction, controls, metrics, and interpretation works structurally.","R1.6":"The artifact contains repeated phrasing and sentence-level style issues, making writing polish a central expected responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection of a broad opinion should preserve uncertainty about the intended evaluation depth and about evidence not present in the excerpt.","RX.2":"The response space must avoid endorsing stronger conclusions than the reported protocol, accuracy range, and correlation can support.","RX.3":"The delegation is broad but still limited to projecting likely responsibilities rather than performing the requested critique or rewrite.","RX.4":"Assessment responsibilities include avoiding advice that could encourage misleading scientific claims or unsupported evaluator conclusions.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the specific delegation wording and the concrete elements present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: A general reaction to the passage would centrally involve judging whether the protocol and contribution are framed clearly at the conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation invites assessment of whether the methodological description and interpretive conclusion follow coherently from the stated setup.
rationale.R1.3: Because the excerpt reports accuracy ranges and a correlation-based implication, judging whether the claims are aligned with the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.5: A general opinion on a draft section includes whether the ordering of protocol, dataset construction, controls, metrics, and interpretation works structurally.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact contains repeated phrasing and sentence-level style issues, making writing polish a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a broad opinion should preserve uncertainty about the intended evaluation depth and about evidence not present in the excerpt.
rationale.RX.2: The response space must avoid endorsing stronger conclusions than the reported protocol, accuracy range, and correlation can support.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is broad but still limited to projecting likely responsibilities rather than performing the requested critique or rewrite.
rationale.RX.4: Assessment responsibilities include avoiding advice that could encourage misleading scientific claims or unsupported evaluator conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the specific delegation wording and the concrete elements present in the artifact.
